source.title: Anterior cingulate activity and heart rate variability in major depression: an intermediate phenotype perspective on autonomic dysregulation

confidence_rationale: Provides the strongest direct evidence that perigenual/subgenual ACC connects bidirectionally with brainstem autonomic effectors on fast timescales, via PAG and medulla. Evidence for fast-effector route is clean. Evidence for SD-032e's slow-baseline write path is INDIRECT — the paper establishes the anatomical substrate for fast autonomic control but does not isolate a slow-drift pathway. 'Mixed' direction because the paper supports an alternative architecture (fast effector write) competitive with the slow-baseline framing SD-032e adopts.
failure_signatures: If REE agents under sustained z_harm_a exposure primarily show fast-timescale autonomic-analog oscillations rather than slow drive_level drift, the correct write target may be a fast-effector module SD-032e has not scoped.

If SD-032e's slow-EMA write succeeds behaviourally but fast autonomic variables (heart-rate-analog / precision / arousal) are unperturbed in exactly the regimes where Gianaros 2011 predicts they should be, the implementation has routed around a genuinely fast pathway.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Perigenual and subgenual ACC show reliable functional coupling with brainstem autonomic effectors (periaqueductal grey, medulla) during stress and emotional challenge. Individual differences in pgACC/sgACC-brainstem coupling predict heart rate variability and baroreflex sensitivity, especially in depression.
mapping.ree_translation: pACC/sgACC has two viable output paths: a fast autonomic-effector route (ACC-PAG-medulla-heart on sub-second to seconds timescale) and a slow setpoint-write route (ACC to interoceptive/valence baseline on minutes-to-hours timescale). SD-032e chose the slow route. Gianaros 2011 provides direct evidence for the fast route and is silent on the slow route. Both routes can coexist biologically; SD-032e's scoping choice is defensible but not unique.
mapping.mapping_caveat: This paper evidences a DIFFERENT architecture than SD-032e proposes. It does not falsify SD-032e — the two routes are compatible biologically — but it shows that any complete cingulate-autonomic substrate will eventually need a fast-effector pathway too. REE currently has no fast-autonomic analogue (no heart-rate-variability-analogue variable, no PAG-analogue module). Using Gianaros to support SD-032e's slow-baseline write is mis-citing the paper.
mapping.source_context: Human fMRI in resting state and under stress tasks, with concurrent ECG. Mixed-age adult samples, with and without depression.

confidence_components.notes: Good source quality. Mapping fidelity low because the paper evidences an alternative (fast-effector) architecture rather than the slow-baseline one SD-032e adopts. Included in this scoping review specifically to document that the fast-effector route exists in the literature and must be explicitly scoped out of SD-032e rather than silently ignored.
